top_categories.workout_context: 186 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 186 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 186 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
186 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,300ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.

race_overlay.heat: June in Flensburg, Germany to Viborg, Denmark can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 186 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 186 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Hærvejsløbet — The Viking Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 186 miles of mixed terrain in Flensburg, Germany to Viborg, Denmark.
